name: Jule Styne
abstract: Jule Styne ( JOO-lee; born Julius Kerwin Stein; December 31, 1905 – September 20, 1994) was an English-American songwriter and composer widely known for a series of Broadway musicals, including several famous frequently-revived shows that also became successful films: Gypsy, Gentlemen Prefer Blondes, and Funny Girl.

works.t: Wake Up and Dream!: What Is This Thing Called Love?

concerts.label: Give My Regards to Broadway – A Salute to 125 Years of Musical Theater

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t meet Arlen, but I met Jule Styne and Burton Lane and to actually meet the songwriters and go to their apartment. And I remember going up to Jule Styne’s apartment and he gave us the song that he kind of, one of the last songs that he’d written and he said we could use it on the album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Jule Styne, you just left us, you know, you just go on and on. One great one after another.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
